subject: Statistics
title: Anomaly Detection in Time Series
description: A micro-lesson that makes Anomaly Detection in Time Series usable.

hook.text: A server's response time spikes from 50ms to 500ms at 3 AM. An anomaly detector sends an alert before users notice.

buildup.text: Anomaly detection identifies data points that deviate significantly from expected patterns.

discovery.text: The model learns normal behavior and flags anything that falls far outside expected bounds.

twist.text: The hard part isn't detecting anomalies — it's reducing false alarms. Too many alerts and people ignore them all.

climax.text: Anomaly detection monitors servers, fraud transactions, industrial sensors, and vital signs — all in real time.

punchline.text: Learn what's normal. Everything else is a signal. Speed is life.

quiz.question: What is the main practical challenge in time series anomaly detection?
options: Reducing false alarms — too many alerts cause people to ignore all warnings
Detecting anomalies is impossible in real time
Anomalies never occur in real-world data
